mnemonics: DOM is like a FAMILY TREE: The HTML document is the parent, and elements like <body> and <div> are its children and grandchildren, all connected in a hierarchical structure.
keyPoints: The DOM is a programming interface that represents an HTML or XML document as a tree of nodes, allowing scripts to dynamically access and update the content, structure, and style of a document.
It provides a structured, object-oriented representation of the document, enabling languages like JavaScript to interact with the page.
Changes to the DOM are reflected in the browser's rendering of the page, making it the core of dynamic web content.
commonMistakes: Confusing the DOM (the live, programmable representation) with the raw HTML source code (the static file).
Inefficiently querying the DOM repeatedly instead of storing a reference to a needed element in a variable, which can cause performance issues.
